TextENGNew: "Hey Otobe, if you're coming, bring the flashlight...!"
"Ah... okay."
Otobe turned back to retrieve the flashlight he'd left on the shoe rack in the assembly hall.
Meanwhile, I chased after Sonozaki-san.
What I'd been feeling strongly for a while now was... that Sonozaki-san knew something about what was happening.
If that was the case, I wouldn't leave her side until she told me what she knew.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: And I had the nagging sense that if I let my guard down for even a second, something else would happen.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: I had no idea what that something might be... but I was certain I couldn't afford to show Sonozaki-san any weakness.
"...Arakawa-saaan! Mion-saaan!"
Otobe ran toward us with a flashlight in hand.
The dim light swayed up and down, left and right, illuminating the area around us. Seeing it, Sonozaki-san furrowed her brow slightly.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: "Otobe.@k@vS10/43/410400338. Point that thing down."
Otobe stared blankly at Sonozaki-san, his expression clearly asking 'why?' Without a word, she walked over, grabbed his hand, and pushed the flashlight beam downward.
"Be careful.@k@vS10/43/410400340. Careless behavior like that will only shorten your lifespan."
After tossing that warning out, Sonozaki-san turned and began walking once again.
Otobe looked perplexed and turned to me for support.
Sure enough, it's difficult to search for anything if you can't see beyond your own feet.
No... wait a minute.
In other words, Sonozaki-san didn't want to illuminate our surroundings... Was it because she didn't want someone to know our location...?
And... who could that someone be?
Enough of that. Thinking wildly is even less productive than not thinking at all.
As I thought that, I continued walking and urged Otobe forward.

When we reached the base of the stone steps, the road extended to the left and right. The road to the right led back the way we came from, and the road to the left headed toward the center of the village.
"Uhh, which way...?"
Sonozaki-san began walking to the left without saying a word.
The rest of us followed behind her.
I couldn't hear anything but the sound of the rain striking the trees and our footsteps on the muddy road.
No matter how far we walked down that straight, pitch-black road, I couldn't see any cars.
"...You parked pretty far away, huh?"
"That's funny.@k@vS10/43/410400343. I don't remember it being this far away..."
Wait a minute, there really is a car, right...?
Her carefree tone only heightened my doubts.
Was Sonozaki-san simply trying to lure us to a remote location...?
And was her goal... to dispose of us?
...This was no time to be joking around. I shook my head to stave off my unpleasant thoughts.
"I really can't find it.@k@vS10/43/410400345. Well, maybe my car was demoned away, too.@k@vS10/43/410400346. ...Hehehe."
"Hey, come on...@k@vS10/62/410101156. This really isn't the time to be making weird jokes."
"Uh... Sonozaki-san.@k@vS10/41/410200504. You really did drive here, didn't you...?"
"Of course.@k@vS10/43/410400348. Even you are saying these things, Otobe?"
"Well... No, I'm sorry."
"Why did you even go to that shrine in the first place?"
"Didn't I already tell you?@k@vS10/43/410400350. I had business in the ritual storehouse."
The ritual storehouse was the building housing all of Hinamizawa's ritualistic tools. ...But they were rituals in name only. The tools were used for torture.
What on earth was she trying to do in a place like that...?
"So you had something to do at the ritual storehouse... I'm asking you to be more specific about what this business was."
"You're very insistent on prying. Are you really that kind of person?"
The frustration in Sonozaki-san's tone essentially told me to "shut up" without actually saying it.
However... I wasn't about to bend to her will. ...Because if I went silent any time that sort of thing happened, I wouldn't be much of a writer.
I stretched my bravado as hard as I could and continued pressuring her.

"Unfortunately, I am actually the kind of person to inquire about the things I find unusual.@k@vS10/62/410101161. But the truth is, we've been caught up in some sort of incident, so I just want to clear up the things I've been feeling anxious about."
"...Would you mind telling me just a little bit more?"
"...You're better off not knowing."
"W-what is that supposed to mean...?"
"I mean exactly what I said."
Sonozaki-san's answer was curt.
Her expression was austere. She seemed intent on not saying anything more. Then, she sealed her lips and continued walking silently.
That very moment, a flashlight began swaying erratically.
"What's wrong, Otobe?"
"Just now... I'm certain something was out there..."
Otobe pointed his flashlight left and right, searching for something. There was an old tire laying on the side of the road for who knows how long.
"Something? What did you see?"
"Just now... you heard a sound, didn't you?"
"A sound...?"
Otobe and I turned our flashlights toward the forest. However, our lights couldn't reach the recesses of the forest.
They could only vaguely illuminate the country road and the weeds growing beside it.
"Is there... someone out there...?"
"Probably your imagination.@k@vS10/43/410400355. ...Let's keep moving."
"Ah, hey..."
Sonozaki-san paid it little heed and continued walking.
...Though, I didn't feel any signs of human presence either.
"Arakawa-san..."
"...Let's keep going for now.@k@vS10/62/410101169. No matter how hard we look, we won't find anything wildly waving our flashlights around."
"It's fine, let's go."
Though Otobe continued to hesitate, I turned my back and kept walking.
I could vaguely make out some of the houses as we traveled down the road. It looked like a small community where a few residents had lived.
Several houses appeared to be lined up, but I couldn't discern anything about them in this darkness.
Even if someone was lurking in one of those houses... it would be tough to find them.
"That's odd...@k@vS10/43/410400357. I shouldn't have parked this far away..."
"...Really?"
"Yes, really."
As she said that, Sonozaki-san walked down a straight road running between two houses.
The uninhabited houses were all lined up, waiting for us.
"In that case, let's turn back...@k@vS10/41/410200512. The car is gone..."
"That appears to be the case... but why don't we look around a little longer?"
The tone of Otobe's voice made it clear he was ready to give up, but she completely tuned it out and steadily continued walking.
...Hold on, isn't that odd?
I don't know what part of the village we're in, but we've walked a long way from the shrine.
Even if the car was out here, she wouldn't have forgotten where she parked it.
So... in other words... Sonozaki-san brought us out here because she wanted to get us away from the shrine...?
"Let's go back already.@k@vS10/41/410200514. Shouldn't we discuss this with everyone back at the assembly hall?"
"Discuss...?@k@vS10/62/410101173. What is there to discuss?@k@vS10/62/410101174. Without a car, there's no way for us to get out of this village."
"That's true, but...@k@vS10/41/410200516. well... something about this area... feels wrong to me...?"
I don't need you telling me that.
...I grumbled that in my head, but decided to encourage him and tell him he was "overthinking" it, annoyed by the idea of simply accepting it.

"No one's living there, so it only feels that way.@k@vS10/62/410101176. You're overthinking it, getting carried away without enough information to go on.@k@vS10/62/410101177. There's nothing out there. It's just your imagination..."
"That's not it! Because...!"
"Lower your voices."
"O-okay..."
Sonozaki-san continued walking down the road as soon as she saw Otobe nod, frantically surveying her surroundings along the way.
...What on earth is she looking for out here?
There was something... oddly suspicious about Sonozaki-san right now.
I had reached the point where I could no longer quietly follow along.
"Hey, Sonozaki-san...@k@vS10/62/410101179. Just how much farther are you planning on going?"
"It's odd...@k@vS10/43/410400363. The car's not here."
"Right... you wouldn't have parked this far away, would you?"
"I suppose that's true..."
I had a bad feeling about those words.
Maybe... the car really did disappear?
"Let me ask you one more time...@k@vS10/62/410101182. You really did drive here, didn't you?"
"Of course. Take a look.@k@vS10/43/410400366. These are for my car."
Sonozaki-san dangled her car keys right in front of my nose.
A sickeningly colorful keychain jangled from the tip of the keyring.
It certainly looked like a set of car keys...
"So, where did you park?"
"In the grass along the nearest house to the shrine."
"Huh?@k@vS10/62/410101185. ...We walked past that a long time ago, didn't we?!"
"That's why I said it was odd."
As she said that, Sonozaki-san walked by the vacant houses and checked them one by one.
...I understand not finding the car. But why is she checking the houses?
Every house was in shambles. I could see the completely dark and quiet rooms beyond the broken windows.
An open door flailed to and fro in the wind.
...The rain continued falling quietly.
The hinges creaked as a door that shouldn't have had anyone nearby slowly opened.
*clatter*
That moment... Otobe dropped his flashlight, and the surrounding area was swallowed by the darkness.
"Ah, sorry...!"
Otobe knelt down and grabbed the flashlight in a panic, but he had trouble getting it to light up. He fiddled with the switch, but it wouldn't turn on.
"What's the matter...?"
"Sorry...@k@vS10/41/410200522. Looks like... it's broken...."
"What have you done!?"
"Sorry...!"
"Ah, I didn't mean that..."
I got caught up in the moment and started yelling.
After all, I was having a hard enough time just walking around a ghost town with a mysterious woman.
But to have our light stop working on top of that...?!
The fear and anxiety blended together inside my heart, culminating in a vague sense of unease. I immediately began to regret lashing out at Otobe.
"Sorry...@k@vS10/62/410101190. ...I shouldn't have raised my voice."

"No, I'm the one who screwed up.@k@vS10/41/410200525. I'm sorry...@k@vS10/41/410200526. But... you really did hear something... didn't you?@k@vS10/41/410200527. ...Like the sound of occasional footsteps from people running around..."
"Footsteps...?"
I listened carefully.
The sound of rain and wind...
And, mixed together with that...!
I turned in the direction of the sound.
It was a house.
Nobody should be living there, but I'm certain I heard a sound just now.
"Probably a fox or a tanuki.@k@vS10/43/410400370. Why don't we go check it out?"
Sonozaki-san started walking toward the source of the sound. In her hand... she firmly clenched the bag containing her katana.
Then she stopped... and listened very carefully.
"...It's just as Otobe says. There's something out there."
"Huh...? Like what...?"
"Could be... a rat."
That moment... a shadow moved across the house.
"Quiet!"
Sonozaki-san held a hand over Otobe's mouth to silence him.
I'm certain... that was the shadow of a person.
A black shadow... inside that house.
Impossible... this is an abandoned village... But that was definitely a person...!
My knees were trembling.
"You too, Arakawa. Quiet down."
Then Sonozaki-san unsheathed her katana and walked toward the house where the shadowy figure lurked.
"S... Sonozaki-san..."
"Arakawa-san... what should we do...?!"
"Hell if I know!"
We kept a close eye on Sonozaki-san's surroundings as she walked down the road.
...Her cool expression made it clear she didn't need watching over. There wasn't much we could do to "protect" her.
I couldn't help but tremble as I watched...
Sonozaki-san stopped in front of a small two-story wooden house. The tin awning on the roof was peeling off, but the front door was still firmly attached.
Still clenching her sword... Sonozaki-san kicked the door open in a single forceful blow.
The surrounding area was still quiet.
Sonozaki-san carefully stepped inside the pitch-dark house.
Otobe and I each held our breath as we watched her go.
After a while, Sonozaki-san came back looking disappointed.
"There's nobody here."
"That can't be?!@k@vS10/41/410200531. I'm sure I saw something!"
"I saw it too.@k@vS10/43/410400378. I'm certain someone went into this house.@k@vS10/43/410400379. ...But, they're not there now."
"What...? What the hell is going on...?"
Sonozaki-san put her sword away. However... she muttered something as she did.
"...@k@vS10/43/410400381.Our enemies are on the move, no doubt about it."
"Enemies...?!"
"...I can't say anything more."
"Hold up!@k@vS10/62/410101202. Why can't you tell us?!@k@vS10/62/410101203. This is important!"
"Quiet down.@k@vS10/43/410400384. ...Do you want the demons to kill you?"
"D... demons...?!"
Otobe, Sonozaki-san, and I stood in the middle of the country road, carefully listening to our surroundings.
*thump* *thump*
Once again, I heard noises coming from one of the houses.
"...What should we do?"
"What should we do...? What's that supposed to mean?"
"Hmph...@k@vS10/43/410400387. You guys... might very well die."
Sonozaki-san looked Otobe and I each directly in the eyes as she said that with a serious look on her face.
...It's hard to tell how serious she's being when she uses words like "die."
It certainly doesn't seem like she's joking around... but are we seriously in a situation with a risk of death...?
What the hell for? How high is the risk? ...It was completely beyond all imagination.
It seemed pointless to think about it any further when I clearly had no clue what was going on.
"I'll clean up the rats here.@k@vS10/43/410400389. ...What are you guys going to do?"
"Alright then, we'll head back!@k@vS10/62/410101207. To the assembly hall, or somewhere!"
"But Arakawa-san...@k@vS10/41/410200534. In the end... we don't have a car, you know...?"
His comment stung, and I choked on my words.
"...That's right.@k@vS10/43/410400391. Sorry, but I think our only option is to walk home."
"What about Towada-san and the others...? Will they be all right...?"
Those few words from Otobe made me catch my breath.
That's right... Miyuki-chan! She's still back at the assembly hall with Towada-san.
Miyuki-chan should be nursing Towada-san right now, but... what if the "rats" Sonozaki-san mentioned had made their way to the assembly hall?
Anxiety quickly fired up as I thought about Miyuki-chan.
If those "rats" knew that two defenseless girls were inside, they'd attack without hesitation, wouldn't they...?
"I'm... going to head back to the assembly hall.@k@vS10/62/410101210. I'm worried about the others."
"Alright, sounds good.@k@vS10/43/410400393. How about you, Otobe?"
"I'll... go with Arakawa-san!"
"Got it.@k@vS10/43/410400395. I'll head there later."
"Alright, Mion-san.@k@vS10/41/410200538. See you later..."
We ran back the way we came in a hurry.
I occasionally tripped over my own feet and stumbled.
Without our flashlight, it was difficult to walk down the road just by moonlight.
But the darkness wasn't the only thing making it difficult to walk.
I was terrified...!
Some unknown creature was wandering around this village, aiming to take our lives...
That alone was enough to petrify me.
"Arakawa-san..."
"Idiot!@k@vS10/62/410101212. Don't try holding my hand!"
"But... it's dark and hard to navigate...!"
"That doesn't mean we need to hold hands!"
We moved at a quick pace and made it back before we knew it.
When we came this way from the shrine it seemed to go on forever, but coming back took no time at all.
"Ah, here it is, Arakawa-san!"
"Yeah! ...Ugh?!"
As I rushed back in a hurry... my foot slipped on a patch of mud and I tumbled down on my butt.
"Oww!"
"Arakawa-san... Please be careful..."
I wiped the mud off my pants as I rose to my feet.
"Are you all right?"
"Sorry about that.@k@vS10/62/410101217. Let's get going."
Just then, I noticed the rain had stopped and I could see the moon between the clouds.
As I climbed the stone steps illuminated by moonlight, I heard Otobe faintly shout "Eek?!" from behind me.
"What's wrong...?"
"Arakawa-san... it's on... your pants..."
I turned to look at the butt of my pants.
...I had a bad feeling about it.
Was it just mud? ...But then, he started to speak up.
"That's... That's blood, isn't it...?!"
It was dark and hard to tell.
So, I cautiously moved both my hands closer to my eyes... and then I froze.
...Though they were totally soaked, I could tell it was something other than mud. With a distinct aroma like rusted iron...
And it was on both of my hands...?!
"Uwah?!@k@vS10/62/410101222. W... When did that get there...?!"
Otobe stared at me with a terrified look in his eyes as he slowly backed down the stone steps, one stair at a time.
...I did fall down just now.
And then I wiped the mud away with my hands.
So, that puddle of mud... was actually... a pool of blood...!
Otobe turned to look away, then dashed into the thicket to get away from me as fast as he could.
"Hey, Otobe?!@k@vS10/62/410101227. Come on, wait up!"
Otobe's silhouette was completely swallowed by the darkness. Once again, I was surrounded by silence.
Who... did this blood come from...?
And why... was there a pool of blood out here...?
"...What about Miyuki-chan?!"
Miyuki-chan and Towada-san were still in the assembly hall at the top of the stone steps.
No way. Did something happen...?!
I charged up the steps at full speed.
Something is going on at this shrine...!
Miyuki-chan, Towada-san...! Please be safe... both of you...!
I dashed up the steps like mad, then ran through the shrine gate and headed straight for the assembly hall.
The light in the assembly hall was still on.
"Miyuki-chan! Towada-san!"
I stepped inside without waiting for a reply.
A fishy stench struck my nose.
Miyuki-chan was lying down in the entry hall, with half her face obscured by her hair.
Blood was steadily pouring out from her stomach.
Making periodic dripping sounds, like a leaky faucet.
This is real... This really happened!
"Miyuki-chan!!"
I knelt down to check Miyuki-chan's face.
Her lips were pale. ...I put a hand on her neck to feel for a pulse, but her body was cold and firm... I understood that she was already gone.
This can't be true... Why? Why did Miyuki-chan have to die...?!
I tried to gently lay Miyuki-chan's corpse down... but then I suddenly stopped.
I saw something in Miyuki-chan's hands... A handgun...?
I tried to pick it up, but rigor mortis had already set in, so I couldn't loosen the grip on her hands.
Why does she... have a handgun...?
The gun in Miyuki-chan's hand felt heavy... and the aroma of gunpowder wafted from the muzzle.
Perhaps... this gun belongs to Miyuki-chan?
Was she carrying this when she came to the village with me...?
Miyuki-chan...
Just who were you...?
I quietly lowered Miyuki-chan's hand and took a look around.
The shoe rack was trashed. Maybe they were searching the house? All the closet and cabinet doors were open and their contents spilled out, too.
Who did this...? And for what purpose...?
I struggled to soothe my throbbing heart as I stepped into the main room.
There were deep red footprints on the tatami mat.
They were big... 26-27 centimeters long...
These are... men's footprints...
Immediately after I entered the assembly room, I stopped in my tracks.
The closet was open and its contents spilled out.
And, right next to the mountain of futons... was another corpse...
"Towada-san...!"
Towada-san was lying on the ground on her back. A huge amount of blood was flowing out of her neck, dying the futons next to her a deep red...
And... looking closer, I noticed another corpse in the back of the room.
No way... Is this who attacked Towada-san and Miyuki-chan...?
Miyuki-chan resisted with her handgun... and fled into the other room... where she took her dying breath...?
Who the hell did this other corpse belong to...?!
I cautiously approached the other corpse.
*bang* *bang*
I heard a dry sound like a firecracker going off, coming from the shrine grounds.
The next moment.
*bang*
I heard the sound again, and a window in the assembly hall shattered that very moment.
The bullet hit the lightbulb, so the interior was bathed in darkness in a flash.
"Who's there...?!"
I shouted in the most intimidating voice I could muster. Immediately after that,
*bang* *bang* *bang*
For a moment, it felt like I heard the sound of the wind.
I felt something hot between my eyebrows... and then everything went black.
The whole thing happened so fast.
They must have fired in the direction of my voice.
My voice... literally killed me.
I can't believe this is where I die...

And so, with still no idea who or what Sonozaki-san was, without learning the truth behind Miyuki-chan or any of the other people I met here, and failing to unravel any of the mysteries found here, my consciousness faded into oblivion.
"I'll wait here."
"Very well.@k@vS10/43/410400399. ...How about you, Otobe?"
Otobe was still hesitating, looking back and forth between Sonozaki-san and me. And then,
"I'll stay here too..."
"Very well... That's good."
Just after saying that, Sonozaki-san slipped off her sandals and returned to the assembly room.
"Sonozaki-san...?"
"Hold on to this... just in case."
Sonozaki-san pulled something from her kimono and sat it in my hand.
It was... small, cold, and surprisingly heavy.
I looked down at my hand, trying to discern what I was holding... and then froze.
"What is this...?@k@vS10/62/410101241. Why a handgun...?!"
"Just a precaution.@k@vS10/43/410400403. If something happens, you'll be prepared."
"If something happens...?!@k@vS10/62/410101243. Like what...?!"
"When the time comes, you'll understand."
"Wh-what... are you talking about?!"
"I wonder.@k@vS10/43/410400406. I hope you won't have the opportunity to use it... Hehehe!"
Sonozaki-san apparently had nothing else to say, so she walked out of the assembly hall bursting with laughter.
"Sonozaki-san...!"
I looked down at the gun in my hand.
This... isn't a toy, right? It's a real gun?!
I'd seen and heard about them many times in fictional contexts, but this was my first time holding a real one.
After holding a gun for the first time, it felt much heavier than I imagined.
"W-why would Mion-san give you something like that...?"
"...Hell if I know."
Otobe's voice trembled as he stood next to me. I squeezed the gun and gave him that curt reply, then returned to the hall.
